homepage.label: Matrix home server address
homepage.description: Enter the address below as your home server in your Matrix application.

messaging.button: Messages, voice messages, calls and video conferences
messaging.title: Messaging and calls in Matrix

intro.title: Messaging in Element
intro.description: Element supports text messages, voice messages, voice and video calls, and video conferences. These work in any room or direct message conversation.

space.title: Space and rooms
space.description: You will automatically join this server's Space as a member. The Space includes all server members and has pre-created rooms so you can start communicating right away.

rooms.title: Messaging in rooms
rooms.description: Send messages in any room or start a direct message with other members. You can also create new rooms that are either discoverable by all Space members or join-by-invitation only.

calls.title: Voice and video calls
calls.description: Start a voice or video call directly from any room or direct message — convenient for quick conversations without setting up a meeting.

meetings.title: Group video call
meetings.description: Element supports high-quality group video calls in rooms — fully equivalent capability to Microsoft Teams, Skype, or other similar services.

encryption.title: Encryption
encryption.description: A key feature of Matrix is strong end-to-end encryption, meaning outsiders, including the server, cannot access your private messages or group messages.

setup.title: Easy primary messaging tool
setup.description: Matrix fulfills all messaging and video call needs well. All members of this server are automatically members of your server's Space, allowing you to start communicating with your team immediately.

rooms.title: Example of a good room structure
rooms.description: In addition to the automatically created rooms, we recommend creating the following rooms, for example, and inviting the appropriate members to them. The core idea is to think the Rooms as radio channels.

command.title: Command room
command.description: Invite your team leaders to the Command channel. The channel functions like a radio command channel — it's for orders and their acknowledgements. It can also be used for briefings via video conference.

friendly.title: Friendly situation room
friendly.description: Invite at least your team leaders and deputy leaders to the Friendly situation room. The channel is used to communicate the execution of your mission — where friendlies are moving and how task execution is progressing.

recon.title: Recon room
recon.description: Invite at least your team leaders and deputy leaders to the Recon room. Use the channel to communicate reconnaissance and enemy observations.

guard.title: Guard room
guard.description: The Guard room should be set up so that anyone can freely join from the Space. Operators starting guard duties or patrol duties join for the duration of their task, giving the command post a direct line to them.

others.title: Other room recommendations
others.description: Other recommended rooms include, among others, a Call for Fires and Fires Management rooms for fire direction and fire coordination.

users.title: User management
users.description: A user set as an administrator in the Deploy App automatically receives administrator rights to the Space and its rooms, and is added to the admin room. A user removed from the Deploy App loses access to Matrix.

federation.title: Cooperation with other Matrix servers
federation.description: By default, you can invite users from other Matrix servers to your rooms. This enables cooperation with other teams using Deploy App or ti NATO allies who also use Matrix, for example through the NI2CE application.

onboarding.completionDesc: Everything is ready and you are good to go.

desktop-intro.title: Welcome to Matrix
desktop-intro.description: This guide uses the Element application, but you can use any Matrix application. The steps are similar in other applications as well.

setup.title: Prerequisites
setup.description: Copy the home server address (1). You can return to this guide at any time from the bottom right corner (2).

desktop-step-2.title: Log in to Element - 2
desktop-step-2.description: Press "Edit" next to the home server address.

desktop-step-3.title: Log in to Element - 3
desktop-step-3.description: Paste the server address you copied earlier. Press "Continue".

desktop-step-4.title: Log in to Element - 4
desktop-step-4.description: Press "Continue with Keycloak". Continue in your browser, which should prompt for your mTLS certificate a few times. Authenticate with your mTLS certificate.

desktop-step-5.title: Element home view
desktop-step-5.description: You are now logged in and can use Matrix. You can view or create Spaces from the sidebar (1). You can see and create direct messages from the left panel (2).

mobile-intro.title: Welcome to Matrix
mobile-intro.description: This guide uses the Element Classic application, but you can use almost any Matrix application. The steps are similar in other applications as well.

mobile-step-2.title: Log in to Element Classic - 2
mobile-step-2.description: Press "Edit" next to the home server address.

mobile-step-3.title: Log in to Element Classic - 3
mobile-step-3.description: Paste the server address you copied earlier. Press "Next".

mobile-step-4.title: Log in to Element Classic - 4
mobile-step-4.description: Press "Continue with Keycloak". Continue in your browser, which should prompt for your mTLS certificate. Authenticate with your mTLS certificate.

mobile-step-5.title: Element Classic home view
mobile-step-5.description: You are now logged in and can use Matrix. You can search for users and rooms from the top right corner (1). You can start a conversation from the bottom right corner (2).

onboarding.title: Matrix onboarding
